clusters.role: Domain-specific design, interface, frontend, and media capability sources
clusters.representativeRule: Do not choose a whole-cluster winner without a bound domain demand; compare only the smallest relevant component when that demand appears.

clusters.role: Autonomous loop, installer, marketplace, and cross-Agent Skill management tooling
clusters.representativeRule: Retain as external tooling or architecture baselines; require a concrete CC Switch or Harness gap and separate executable or Hook review before any use.

clusters.role: Large heterogeneous Skill and plugin collections spanning many task domains
clusters.representativeRule: Never admit or compare the suite as one payload; split only the component selected by a concrete residual gap.

clusters.role: Skills whose useful behavior depends materially on external services, APIs, accounts, or credentials
clusters.representativeRule: Hold until a bound service task, account and data boundary, license provenance, authority, and verification surface exist.

sources.reason: Six permissively licensed Skill bodies and no detected executable or Hook surface make this a low-friction future comparator, but the current Harness demand batch does not select design quality.
No SKILL.md was detected and 31 executable files require a tooling review; its loop role overlaps the already compared Loopy and current Harness paths.
The suite is explicitly Stitch MCP-dependent and includes 19 executable files and seven plugin manifests, so a concrete service and project demand must precede component review.
Sixty-eight Skill bodies and ten executable files provide breadth but not a defensible whole-suite comparison or admission unit.
The preflight found no license artifact or metadata and the suite is service-oriented; no current account, data, or authority boundary selects it.
Forty-four Skill bodies, 18 executable files, and 23 plugin manifests require component-level runtime, permission, and overlap review.
The single permissively licensed Skill has no detected executable or Hook surface, but current evidence does not select a design-quality demand.
The large host and web-stack suite includes 36 executable files and 12 plugin manifests; current human-AI Harness evidence does not justify whole-suite review.
The installer-oriented suite includes 64 Skill bodies, 18 executable files, one plugin manifest, and one Hook surface that cannot be admitted as one unit.
The preflight found no license artifact or metadata and current demand evidence does not select Remotion or video production.
Two Skill bodies sit inside a much larger project repository with 3,801 executable files; only an exact component and project demand can bound a review.
The preflight found no license artifact or metadata and the repository has 165 executable files; current demand does not select a Vercel-specific component.
Prior pinned review identifies an operational discovery, install, update, use, and removal CLI that overlaps CC Switch management and the retired custom Manager experiment.
The marketplace-scale source contains 175 Skill bodies, 64 executable files, 184 plugin manifests, and two Hook surfaces; breadth increases review cost rather than proving marginal Harness value.

representativeSelection.reason: The complete 62-row coordinate envelope currently has zero supported residual gaps; cluster breadth alone is not a demand signal.
representativeSelection.futureRule: Select the smallest source component that matches a newly bound residual gap, then compare it with native, official/runtime, composed, non-Skill Harness, project-standard, and human-authority paths.

marginalYieldHistory.yield: five capability clusters and nine representative sources; later query expansion produced mostly variants and noise
five sources selected for source-pinned static review and no approved candidate
four overlaps and sixteen new direct coordinates
fourteen available sources expanded domain and suite breadth, two stale coordinates were resolved, and no new current Harness shortfall class or demand-linked review batch emerged

stopDecision.reason: Additional source count is no longer producing a new current Harness shortfall class or a supported residual gap; continuing without a bound demand would increase review cost and executable, Hook, license, account, and overlap surface without improving the decision.

remainingUncertainty: The public ecosystem continues to change and this evidence is not an exhaustive catalog.
Structure preflight does not establish body quality, behavioral effectiveness, safety, portability, or superiority.
Large suites can contain valuable individual components that are invisible at repository-level classification.
Four available sources still lack license evidence at the pinned preflight revision.
Consumer, cross-Agent, longitudinal, and production evidence remains outside this bounded discovery result.
recheckTriggers: A new source-supported human-AI collaboration demand or reproducible residual failure is bound.
A concrete design, frontend, media, service, account, or project domain task selects one cluster.
CC Switch, the active runtime, Agent loading, or Hook behavior changes materially.
A pinned source revision, license, security, maintenance, or availability signal changes.
Consumer feedback or paired evidence shows that the current path is insufficient or materially costlier.

acceptanceAssessment.scope: bounded discovery round only; not ecosystem completeness, candidate approval, runtime effectiveness, or permanent stop
